frameworks.name: Support Vector Machine classification
frameworks.one_line: linear SVM classifies tDCS responders from individualized electric field features

frameworks.name: MRI-derived electric field modeling
frameworks.one_line: person-specific finite-element current models quantify delivered field

frameworks.name: precision cognitive intervention
frameworks.one_line: framing for individualized stimulation optimization in older adults

core_question: Can individual variability in MRI-derived tDCS electric fields predict working-memory improvement after stimulation?
key_assumptions: delivered electric field varies meaningfully across individuals
field characteristics near stimulation electrodes drive cognitive response
responder status is a learnable function of current features
manipulations: individual electric field components derived from MRI models
responder vs non-responder grouping based on N-back change
tDCS at F3/F4 paired with two-week cognitive training
key_findings: SVM on current features classified responders vs non-responders with 86% accuracy
median current intensity near electrodes positively related to working-memory gain
current direction near electrodes also related to intervention response
interpretations: individual electric-field variability is a tractable predictor of tDCS cognitive benefit
field-informed targeting could enable person-specific tDCS optimization
limitations_noted: small pilot sample of fourteen healthy older adults
proof-of-concept design rather than clinical trial
